Help & Support

Everything you need to understand the Offlode platform. Each section below walks through a key area in the order you'll encounter it on screen.

What is Offlode?

The 60-second overview for anyone new to the platform.

Offlode is an AI-powered client operations platform for accounting and bookkeeping firms. It handles the repetitive client communication that consumes your day — chasing documents, managing callbacks, sending invoice reminders — so you can focus on the work that actually needs a human.

The platform has three AI agents that work on your behalf:

Documents — automatically chases missing documents from clients
Calls — manages callbacks, enquiries, and meeting requests
Payments — handles invoicing, payment reminders, and disputes

These agents are powered by Synapse, Offlode's AI intelligence engine. Synapse monitors your firm's data, identifies what needs attention, takes action where it can, and escalates to you when it needs a human decision.

Your job is simple: check the Workspace each morning, handle the items Synapse has flagged, and move on. Everything else runs automatically.

Three roles exist in Offlode. Owner has full platform control — billing, subscription management, adding and removing any user, and all Settings. Admin users can add and remove team members, configure agents, manage team assignments, and access the audit log — but cannot remove the Owner or change billing and subscription. Manager users see their assigned clients' Workspace, Dashboard, Schedule, and Reports — everything needed to do the daily work, without access to firm-wide configuration. All roles have full access to this Help & Support guide.

Quick Start — Your First 5 Minutes

New to Offlode? Follow these five steps to get oriented. You'll be working productively within minutes.

1

Read the Dashboard briefing

When you log in, the Dashboard is your starting point. The ✦ Synapse briefing at the top tells you exactly what needs attention today and who's most urgent. Read it like a morning email from your most organised colleague.

2

Go to the Workspace

Click Workspace in the sidebar (or the button in the briefing). This is where you do your actual work. The left panel shows clients sorted by urgency — the ones at the top need you most.

3

Click the first client

Click the top client in the list. You'll see their command centre — Synapse's AI assessment at the top, then three agent zones (Documents, Calls, Payments) below. The assessment tells you what's going on in one sentence. The zones show what specifically needs doing.

4

Open a task zone

Click any agent zone that has a count badge. You'll enter Focus Mode — a detailed view of every task in that category. Each task shows what happened, why it matters, and what Synapse recommends. Use the action buttons to resolve, approve, or dismiss.

5

Work through the list

When you've handled one client, click Back to return to the list. The next most urgent client is waiting. Keep going until "Needs Input" is empty — that's your inbox zero. Everything else, Synapse handles automatically.

That's it. Offlode is designed so that your Workspace "Needs Input" count is the only number that matters. Get it to zero and you're done. Synapse handles the rest — chasing documents, managing callbacks, sending reminders — and only escalates back to you when it genuinely needs a human decision.

Dashboard

Your firm's operations at a glance. The Dashboard is the first screen you see when logging in and gives you an immediate overview of what needs attention.

1. AI Briefing

✦ Synapse Daily Brief

The teal-accented card at the top of the Dashboard. Offlode's AI engine — Synapse — analyses your firm's data overnight and generates a morning briefing. It tells you how many clients need attention, who is most urgent, and recommends where to start. Includes a one-click button to go directly to the Workspace.

2. KPI Strip

Key Performance Indicators

The row of four metric cards below the briefing. Shows: Documents (collection completion %), Callbacks (overdue count), Outstanding (total £ overdue), and Automation (actions taken by the three AI agents). Each includes a sparkline showing the 7-period trend.

3. Needs Attention

Priority Client List

A ranked list of clients requiring your action, ordered by urgency. Each row shows the client name, their most pressing issue, and which AI agents are involved. Click any row to jump to that client's Workspace. Shows a colour-coded severity indicator for each.

4. Upcoming Deadlines

Deadline Calendar

A compact list of imminent deadlines — VAT returns, year ends, filing dates. Shows the date, client name, and deadline type. Red dates indicate items due within the next 7 days. Click to see the client detail.

Workspace

Your primary working area. The Workspace uses a three-stage flow: triage the list, review the command centre, then deep-focus on individual tasks.

1. Filter Tabs

Needs Input / Handled / VIP

The filter controls at the top of the client list. Needs Input shows clients with pending tasks requiring your action (badge shows count). Handled shows clients where everything is up to date. The VIP toggle filters to starred high-priority clients only.

2. Triage List

Client List

The left panel showing all clients sorted by urgency. Each row displays the client name, their most urgent issue summary, and a task count badge. A red vertical bar indicates action-required severity. Click any client to see their command centre in the middle panel.

3. AI Assessment

✦ Synapse Command Brief

When you select a client, the first thing you see is Synapse's assessment of their situation. This includes the overall status, key risk factors, and a recommended action. The AI has already analysed all their data — documents, calls, payments — and synthesised it into a single actionable summary.

4. Agent Zones

Documents / Calls / Payments

Below the AI assessment, three zone rows represent the AI agents: Documents handles document chasing. Calls manages callbacks and enquiries. Payments handles invoicing and disputes. Each shows a task count and the most urgent item. Click a zone to enter Focus Mode.

5. Focus Mode

Deep Focus

When you click into a zone, the workspace expands. The client list collapses, giving you a full-width view of all tasks in that category. Each task shows the issue, evidence, AI analysis, and action buttons to resolve it. The context panel appears on the right with client details for reference.

6. Context Panel

Client Context

The right sidebar in Focus Mode. Shows contact details, assigned manager, financial summary (outstanding, this year, lifetime value), key dates (VAT quarter end, year end, last contact), and preference tags. Stays visible while you work through tasks so you always have the full picture.

Clients

Your complete client directory. View, search, and manage all client records.

1. Search & Filter

Finding Clients

The search bar at the top filters the list by name as you type. Tab filters work the same as the Workspace — All, Needs Input, and VIP. The client count appears at the bottom of the list.

2. Client Detail

Client Profile

Click any client to see their full profile in the right panel. Shows contact information, assigned manager, active agent status, document sets, financial summary, and a complete activity history. Use the tabs to switch between different data views.

Team

Manage your firm's team members and their workload.

1. Team List

Team Members

Shows all team members with their role, assigned client count, and active task summary. Click any row to open the detail panel from the right, showing their full workload breakdown, recent activity, and performance metrics.

2. Assignments

Client Assignments

Each team member has clients assigned to them. The assignment determines who reviews and acts on AI-escalated tasks for that client. Reassigning is done through the Settings page under Team Management.

Schedule

View and manage all scheduled events — callbacks, meetings, deadlines, and AI-scheduled actions.

1. Date Navigation

Day Selector

The header shows the current date with arrow buttons to navigate between days. The "Today" button jumps back to the current date. A page counter shows how many events are scheduled for the selected day.

2. Time Slots

Day View

The main area shows hourly time slots with events positioned at their scheduled times. Events are colour-coded: purple for callbacks, blue for meetings, amber for deadlines, red for disputes, and teal for AI-scheduled actions.

3. Event Detail

Event Panel

Click any event to open the detail panel on the right. Shows full details: client, type, time, duration, assigned team member, and context notes. Action buttons let you mark as complete, reschedule, or log notes. Events with ✦ are AI-generated by Synapse.

Reports

Analytics and insights across your practice. Five tabs covering different aspects of firm performance.

1. ✦ Synapse Insight

AI Analysis

At the top of each Reports tab, Synapse provides an AI-generated insight specific to that data set. It identifies the most significant trend, anomaly, or opportunity and recommends an action. This changes as your data evolves.

2. Overview Tab

Firm-Wide Metrics

The default view showing aggregate performance: total agent activity, document completion rates, and response times over 6 months. Area charts visualise trends. KPI cards at the top show headline numbers with period-over-period change.

3. Clients Tab

Client Performance

Per-client rankings and metrics. Document chase effectiveness, overdue rates, compliance scores. Identifies which clients need more attention and which are running smoothly. Sortable table with bar-chart visualisation.

4. Documents / Calls / Payments

Agent-Specific Tabs

Each of the three remaining tabs focuses on a single agent's domain. Documents: submission rates, turnaround times, overdue categories. Calls: callback response times, resolution rates, escalation frequency. Payments: outstanding amounts, payment timelines, dispute rates, collection efficiency.

✦

✦ Synapse — AI Intelligence Engine

Synapse is Offlode's AI engine — named for the neural connections it creates between your firm's data, your clients, and your team. Like a biological synapse transmitting signals between neurons, Synapse connects the dots across documents, calls, and payments to surface what matters and act on your behalf.

Recognition

The ✦ Symbol

Anywhere you see the ✦ symbol, Synapse is at work. This appears on insight panels, automated schedule events, and AI-generated recommendations. It's your signal that the content was produced or curated by Offlode's AI rather than entered manually.

Format

BLUF Signal Format

Synapse uses "Bottom Line Up Front" — the most important conclusion is stated first as a bold headline, followed by supporting evidence. Signal types: ATTENTION (urgent, action required), OPPORTUNITY (potential improvement identified), TREND (emerging pattern worth monitoring).

Three Agents

Documents / Calls / Payments

Synapse operates through three specialised agents. Documents chases missing documents automatically. Calls handles callbacks, enquiries, and meeting requests. Payments manages invoicing, reminders, and dispute resolution. Each works autonomously and escalates to you when human judgement is needed.

Escalation

When Synapse Asks For Help

Not everything can be automated. When Synapse encounters a situation requiring human judgement — a sensitive client issue, an unusual request, a compliance decision — it escalates to your Workspace with full context. You see exactly what happened, why it was flagged, and what Synapse recommends. You decide; Synapse executes.

Settings

Configure your firm's Offlode setup, agent behaviour, and integrations.

1. Firm Profile

Firm Details

Your firm's name, branding, and contact information. Changes here affect how Synapse represents your firm in client communications — email signatures, SMS sender names, and document headers.

2. Agent Config

Synapse Agent Settings

Control how each AI agent behaves. Set escalation thresholds (days before an item is flagged overdue), communication frequency (how often reminders are sent), tone (formal vs friendly), and which actions require your approval before Synapse executes.

3. Integrations

Connected Services

Connect Offlode to your existing tools: Xero/QuickBooks for accounting data, Microsoft 365/Google Workspace for email and calendar, your phone system for call handling. Each integration can be enabled, configured, and tested independently.

4. Audit Log

Activity Audit

A complete record of every action taken in Offlode — both by your team and by Synapse. Shows who did what, when, and for which client. Filterable by user, action type, and date range. Essential for compliance and oversight.

General

Platform-wide features available everywhere in Offlode.

Header Bar

Navigation & Search

The header contains: the Offlode logo (click to return to Dashboard), the current page name, global search (press ⌘K or click), the + create button for quick actions (log a call, send an invoice, add a document set), notifications (bell icon with red badge for unread), and your profile menu (theme switching, sign out).

Sidebar

Main Navigation

The left sidebar provides navigation to all sections. The active page is highlighted with a teal accent bar. The sidebar can be collapsed to a compact icon-only view using the toggle button at the bottom — useful for maximising screen space when working in Focus Mode.

Appearance

Theme Options

Three themes available from your profile menu: Hybrid (dark navigation + light content — the default and recommended option), Dark (fully dark, reduces eye strain), and Light (fully light, high contrast). Your preference is saved automatically.

Support

Getting Help

For assistance beyond this guide, contact your account manager or email support@offlode.com. For urgent issues affecting live client communications, use the priority support line listed on your plan details page in Settings.

Step-by-Step Walkthroughs

Common tasks explained end-to-end. Follow these when you need to do something specific.

Document Chasing

How to handle an overdue document chase

Synapse has been chasing a client for documents but they haven't responded. The task appears in your Workspace as an escalation.

1. Open the Workspace and click the client with the overdue document flag.
2. In the command centre, click the Documents zone to enter Focus Mode.
3. You'll see the document set, how many reminders Synapse has sent, and when the last one went out.
4. Decide: click Send stronger reminder (Synapse sends a firmer email), Call client (logs a callback and opens the call guide), or Extend deadline (pushes the date and pauses reminders).
5. Once actioned, the task clears from your list. Synapse resumes monitoring.

Callbacks

How to manage a callback request

A client has called the firm and left a message, or Synapse has identified a callback is needed.

1. The callback appears in your Workspace under the Calls zone with an urgency indicator.
2. Click into it to see the context: who called, when, what about, and any notes from the initial contact.
3. Click Call now to mark it as in progress (this updates the Schedule and prevents Synapse from sending duplicate reminders).
4. After the call, click Log outcome to record what was discussed and any follow-up actions.
5. If a follow-up is needed, Synapse automatically schedules it and adds it to your Schedule.

Invoicing

How to resolve a disputed invoice

A client has queried or disputed an invoice and Synapse has escalated it to you.

1. Open the client's Payments zone in the Workspace.
2. You'll see the disputed invoice with the client's message/query and Synapse's assessment of the situation.
3. Review the details: invoice amount, what it covers, the client's objection, and payment history.
4. Choose an action: Send clarification (explain the charges), Adjust invoice (issue a credit note or amendment), Escalate internally (assign to a partner or manager), or Mark resolved (client has accepted).
5. Synapse updates the invoice status and resumes payment chasing if appropriate.

Client Onboarding

How to onboard a new client

1. Click the + button in the header and select Add client. If you use Xero or QuickBooks, you can import instead of entering manually.
2. Fill in: client name, legal entity, primary contact, email, phone, assigned manager.
3. Set their preferences: communication tone (formal/friendly), VIP status, any special notes.
4. Create a document set — the list of documents you need to collect from them. Use a template or build from scratch.
5. Toggle on the agents you want active: Documents, Calls, Payments.
6. Save. Synapse immediately begins its initial outreach — sending the first document request and confirming contact details.

Period End

How to run month-end or quarter-end

1. Check the Dashboard — the Synapse brief will flag any clients with upcoming deadlines.
2. Go to Reports → Overview to see firm-wide completion rates. Any clients below target are highlighted.
3. Switch to Reports → Documents to see which document categories have the lowest submission rates.
4. Open the Workspace and work through the "Needs Input" list, prioritising clients with imminent deadlines.
5. Check the Schedule for any deadline-related events Synapse has flagged.
6. Once all deadline clients are handled, check Reports → Payments for any outstanding invoices that need chasing before the period closes.

Add Client Modal

How to add a new client — field by field

Click + in the header → Add client. The modal has several sections:

Business Details — Legal name (required), trading name (if different), entity type (Limited Company, LLP, Sole Trader, etc.), company number, UTR, SIC code, industry, and employee count.

Tax & VAT — VAT registration number, VAT scheme (Standard, Flat Rate, Cash, None), year end date, and corporation tax reference.

Addresses — Registered and trading addresses (can be the same).

Primary Contact (required) — Name, email, phone, role. This is who Synapse communicates with.

Financials & Billing — Annual fee, billing frequency, payment terms, and accounting software (Xero, QuickBooks, etc.).

Services — Which services your firm provides (Accounts, Tax, Payroll, VAT, Bookkeeping, etc.).

Assignment & Relationship — Assigned manager, partner, communication tone (formal/friendly), and VIP status.

Compliance — AML/KYC check status, risk level, and ID verification.

Only Legal name and Primary contact are required — everything else can be filled in later.

New Invoice Modal

How to create an invoice — field by field

Click + in the header → New invoice, or from a client's Payments zone.

Invoice type — Toggle between One-off (single invoice) and Recurring (auto-generates on a schedule). Recurring shows additional fields: frequency (monthly/quarterly/annually), amount type (fixed or variable), start date, and optional end date.

Client & number — Select the client and confirm the auto-generated invoice number.

Line items — Add rows with description, quantity, rate, and VAT rate (20%, 5%, 0%, or Exempt). Click + Add line item for more. Remove with ×.

Totals — Subtotal, VAT, and grand total calculate automatically.

Payment — Payment terms (Net 7/14/30/45/60) and due date. Due date auto-calculates from terms but can be overridden.

Notes — Optional message displayed on the invoice.

Click Send invoice to issue immediately, or Save as draft to finalise later.

Document Set Wizard

How to create a document set — step by step

A document set is the list of documents you need to collect from a client. Click + → Add document set.

Step 1: Choose method — From template (reuse a saved set), Import from Xero (pull from accounting software), Upload CSV (bulk import from spreadsheet), or Build manually (create from scratch).

Step 2: Configure — Select a template, connect to Xero, map CSV columns, or add individual items with names, categories, and deadlines.

Step 3: Assign — Select the client and confirm the assigned manager.

Step 4: Review & create — Preview the set. Click Create to activate. Synapse immediately begins chasing items that are due. You can save your configuration as a template for reuse.

Glossary

Key terms used throughout Offlode, explained plainly.

Synapse

Offlode's AI intelligence engine. Powers all three agents, generates insights, and decides when to escalate to you. Marked with the ✦ symbol throughout the platform.

Agent

An autonomous AI worker that handles a specific domain. Offlode has three: Documents, Calls, and Payments. Agents work independently and escalate when they need human input.

Escalation

When Synapse encounters a situation it can't or shouldn't handle automatically, it "escalates" — placing the task in your Workspace with full context and a recommended action.

Command Centre

The summary view you see when you select a client in the Workspace. Shows Synapse's AI assessment and the three agent zones at a glance.

Focus Mode

The expanded view when you click into a specific agent zone. The client list collapses and you see all tasks in that category with full detail.

Intent Badge

The coloured label on a task showing its current state. Overdue (red) = past deadline. Stalled (amber) = no progress despite reminders. Pending (blue) = waiting for response. Review (teal) = received, needs your check.

Severity

How urgent a client's situation is. Action (red bar) = immediate attention needed. Review (amber) = should look at today. Handled (no bar) = everything is up to date.

Document Set

A defined collection of documents needed from a client — e.g., "Year End 2024" containing bank statements, P60s, and receipts. Synapse tracks each item individually and chases what's missing.

VIP

A starred client who gets priority treatment. VIP clients appear at the top of filtered lists and can have different communication settings (e.g., more formal tone, shorter response windows).

BLUF

"Bottom Line Up Front" — the format Synapse uses for insights. The key conclusion is stated first as a bold headline, with supporting detail below. Designed so you can act on the headline alone if you're in a hurry.

ATTENTION / OPPORTUNITY / TREND

The three types of Synapse signal. ATTENTION = urgent, requires action now. OPPORTUNITY = something could be improved. TREND = an emerging pattern worth watching but not acting on yet.

Troubleshooting

Something not working as expected? Start here.

Synapse did something I didn't expect

Check the Audit Log (profile menu → Audit Log) to see exactly what Synapse did, when, and why. Every automated action is logged with the reasoning. If the behaviour was wrong, adjust the agent's configuration in Settings — you can change escalation thresholds, communication frequency, and which actions require your approval.

I can't find a client, document, or invoice

Press ⌘K (or Ctrl+K) to open global search. Type any part of the client name, document title, or invoice number. If it doesn't appear, check: is the client assigned to you? (Managers only see their assigned clients.) Is the filter set to "All" rather than "Needs Input"? For documents and invoices, they live inside the client's profile — search for the client first.

Data isn't syncing from Xero or QuickBooks

Go to Settings → Integrations and check the connection status. If it shows "Disconnected," click reconnect and re-authorise. If it shows "Connected" but data is stale, check the last sync timestamp — syncs run every 15 minutes. You can force an immediate sync with the "Sync now" button. If issues persist, check that the integration user in Xero/QuickBooks still has the required permissions.

A notification won't clear

Click the notification bell and click "Mark all as read" at the bottom. If a specific notification persists, it may be tied to an unresolved task — clearing the underlying task in your Workspace will clear the notification.

The page looks broken or doesn't load

Try a hard refresh: ⌘+Shift+R (Mac) or Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows). If the issue persists, try a different browser. Offlode is optimised for Chrome, Safari, Firefox, and Edge (latest versions). If you're on a mobile device, the full desktop layout requires a screen width of at least 1024px.

A client says they didn't receive an email from Synapse

Check the client's Activity Timeline (in their profile or Workspace Focus Mode) to confirm the email was sent. If it shows as sent, the issue is likely on the client's end — spam filters are the most common cause. You can resend from the activity entry. If emails consistently fail for a client, check their email address is correct in the client profile.

Frequently Asked Questions

Quick answers to the questions we hear most often.

A client hasn't sent their documents. What do I do?

Nothing — unless Synapse asks you to. The Documents agent automatically sends reminders on a schedule you control (Settings → Agent Configuration). If a client remains unresponsive after multiple chases, Synapse escalates to your Workspace with the full history. At that point, you decide: send a stronger reminder, call them directly, or extend the deadline.

Why did Synapse flag this client as urgent?

Synapse considers multiple factors: how overdue items are, the client's payment history, upcoming deadlines, and whether multiple agents have issues simultaneously. A client with one overdue document might be "review" severity, but a client with overdue documents and an overdue callback and a disputed invoice would be "action" severity. The ✦ insight panel on their command centre explains exactly why.

How do I stop Synapse from sending reminders to a specific client?

Open the client's profile in the Clients page, find the agent controls, and toggle off the agent you want to pause. You can disable Documents, Calls, or Payments individually per client. The agent will stop all automated communication but continue monitoring — so if something urgent happens, it still escalates to you.

What does the red badge on the notification bell mean?

You have unread notifications. Click the bell to see them. Notifications include: new client submissions, completed AI actions, escalated tasks, team mentions, and system alerts. The badge disappears once you've viewed the notification panel.

How do I log a call or callback manually?

Click the + button in the header and select "Log a call." Choose the client, fill in the details, and save. The call is recorded in the client's activity history and Synapse adjusts its scheduling accordingly — for example, if you've just spoken to a client, Synapse won't schedule a reminder for them tomorrow.

Can I change how formal Synapse sounds when emailing clients?

Yes. Go to Settings → Agent Configuration and adjust the tone setting for each agent. Options range from formal (suited to solicitors and corporate clients) to friendly (suited to small businesses and sole traders). You can also set per-client overrides if a specific client prefers a different tone — check their preferences in the client profile.

What happens when I dismiss a Synapse insight?

The insight disappears from the current view. Synapse takes this as a signal that you've noted the information and don't need to act on it right now. If the underlying situation worsens (e.g., a client goes from "slightly overdue" to "significantly overdue"), Synapse will generate a new, more urgent insight. Dismissing is not the same as resolving — it's "acknowledged, not now."

I need to send an invoice. Where do I do that?

Click the + button in the header and select "New invoice." Or from a client's Workspace command centre, click into the Payments zone and use the "Create invoice" button. If you have Xero or QuickBooks connected, invoice data syncs automatically.

How do I add a new client?

Click the + button in the header and select "Add client." Fill in their details — name, contact, email, assigned manager. If you have an accounting integration connected, you can import clients directly from Xero or QuickBooks instead of entering them manually.

What's the difference between Hybrid, Dark, and Light themes?

Hybrid (default) gives you dark navigation with light content — the best of both worlds. Dark is fully dark, reducing eye strain in low-light environments. Light is fully light with high contrast. Switch anytime from your profile menu (top-right avatar). Your choice is saved across sessions.

Is there a keyboard shortcut for search?

Press ⌘K (Mac) or Ctrl+K (Windows) anywhere in Offlode to open the command palette. Type a client name, action, or page name and hit Enter. Press Escape to close any open panel, dropdown, or modal.

Who can see the audit log?

The audit log is available to Owners and Admins (configurable in Settings → Team). It records every action — both human and AI — with timestamps, user attribution, and affected client. This is your compliance trail and can be filtered by date, user, or action type.

Can I undo something Synapse has done?

It depends on the action. Emails already sent cannot be recalled, but you can send a follow-up correction. Scheduled events can be cancelled or rescheduled. Invoice reminders can be paused. Check the Audit Log to see what happened, then adjust the agent settings in Settings to require your approval for that type of action in future.

Can multiple team members work on the same client?

Yes. Each client has one assigned manager who is the primary contact, but any team member with access can view their data and take actions. Managers see their assigned clients by default; Admins and Owners see everyone. The audit log tracks which team member took each action.

How do I export data or reports?

On the Reports page, each tab has an export button (top-right) that generates a CSV or PDF. For client data, open the client profile and use the export option. The Audit Log can also be exported for compliance. All exports respect your permissions — you can only export data you have access to view.

What exactly does Synapse send to my clients?

Synapse sends emails and SMS on your firm's behalf using your branding, tone settings, and firm name. Preview the exact templates in Settings → Agent Configuration → Communication Templates. If you want to approve every outgoing message before it's sent, enable "Require approval" for that agent.

Is my client data secure?

Yes. All data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest (AES-256). Offlode is hosted on SOC 2 compliant infrastructure within the UK. Client data is never shared between firms. Access is controlled by user roles and permissions. The Audit Log provides a complete trail. Full details at offlode.com/security.

How far back does the activity history go?

The activity timeline retains all data for the lifetime of the account — there is no automatic deletion. The Dashboard shows the last 30 days by default but you can adjust the date range. The Audit Log is retained indefinitely for compliance.

What happens if I'm offline or away?

Synapse continues working whether you're logged in or not. Automated reminders, scheduled callbacks, and payment chasing all run on schedule. Escalations queue in your Workspace until you return. If you're away for an extended period, ask your admin to temporarily reassign your clients to a colleague.

How do I preview an email or SMS before Synapse sends it?

When you're in a client's Workspace and click Send or Email, a preview modal opens showing the exact message — recipient, subject line, and body text. You can edit the content before sending, schedule it for later using the "Schedule send" dropdown, or cancel. For calls, the preview shows a call guide with talking points and client context. Nothing sends until you confirm.

How do I invite a new team member?

Go to Settings → Team and click Invite member. Enter their email address and select their role (Admin or Manager). They'll receive an email invitation with a link to set up their account. Owners and Admins can send invitations; Managers cannot. You can revoke a pending invitation before it's accepted.

What are the small notifications that appear at the bottom of the screen?

These are toast notifications — brief confirmation messages that appear when you complete an action (e.g., "Email sent", "Client saved", "Settings updated"). They disappear automatically after a few seconds. Green toasts confirm success; red toasts indicate an error. They don't require any action from you — they're just confirmation that something worked.

What does the red or amber bar at the top of a page mean?

A red banner indicates an error — something went wrong and may need your attention (e.g., a failed sync, a connectivity issue). The banner includes a description and usually a dismiss button. An amber banner is a warning — not critical but worth noting (e.g., "Your subscription renews in 3 days"). Both disappear when dismissed or when the underlying issue is resolved.
